name: Saskatchewan (Canada)

colors.symbolism: the northern boreal forests of Saskatchewan
the southern prairies and wheat fields
the prairie lily and the lion on the shield
outlines on the shield and lily

description: Two equal horizontal stripes of green over yellow; in the upper-hoist quadrant the provincial shield of arms, and in the fly half a stylised western red lily (prairie lily).
history: Adopted on 22 September 1969 following a province-wide design competition won by Anthony Drake of Hodgeville.

features.symbolism: Green represents the northern forested area of the province, and gold the southern grain fields.

features.role: provincial shield of arms (in chief a red lion passant on gold; below, three gold wheat sheaves on green)
features.symbolism: The lion recalls the British Crown; the three gold wheat sheaves on green represent Saskatchewan's primary agricultural product.

features.role: western red lily (prairie lily), the provincial floral emblem
features.symbolism: the prairie lily, Saskatchewan's official floral emblem since 1941
